title: Documentation of *.preview.tsx schema with examples
description: Write reference documentation for the `*.preview.tsx` file contract (the `PreviewConfig` shape from TASK-37), with complete, realistic examples covering variants and each control kind.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/preview-tsx-schema.md` documents every field of `PreviewConfig`, `PreviewVariant`, and `PreviewControl` with type and purpose
Includes at least one complete, realistic example file (e.g. a Button component) with 2+ variants and one control of each supported kind (text, boolean, select)
Documents file discovery/naming convention expected by the connector (`*.preview.tsx`, TASK-39) and the assumed relationship between the preview file and the actual component export
Documents the `postMessage` contract (`systembook:update-props`) for anyone wanting to understand or extend `preview-kit`'s runtime behavior
Cross-linked from TASK-60's setup docs at the connector installation step

steps.details: Write a field-by-field reference table for `PreviewConfig`, `PreviewVariant`, `PreviewControl` sourced from the final types in `packages/schema/src/preview-config.ts` (TASK-37).

Write a full `button.preview.tsx` example with a `default` and `destructive` variant, and controls for `label` (text), `disabled` (boolean), and `size` (select with `sm | md | lg` options), matching the real `PreviewConfig` shape exactly.

Explain the `*.preview.tsx` naming/location convention, and clarify (per TASK-40's assumption) where the connector expects to find the actual React component being previewed relative to the preview file.

Document the exact message shape (`{ type: 'systembook:update-props', props: Record<string, unknown> }`) sent from the admin panel's controls (TASK-49) and consumed by `preview-kit`'s `mount()` (TASK-38), for advanced users who want to build custom control UIs.

steps.description: Cross-link and verify against real implementation
steps.details: Add a link from TASK-60's setup docs to this file. Verify the documented example actually builds and runs correctly through the real connector pipeline (TASK-39-41) before finalizing.

technicalNotes: Keep this document in sync with `packages/schema`'s actual exported types â€” consider generating the field reference table from TSDoc comments in a later iteration, but hand-written is acceptable for the MVP.
